core_mechanism: a falsification-based verification procedure that certifies whether a candidate expression equals a target quantity by searching the constraint-admissible model class for a distinguishing counterexample, yielding an almost-surely-correct decision under a regular exponential-family (finite-parameter) parametrization
brief_reason: The abstract poses verification (does a given formula equal the target?) as distinct from existence/identification and solves it via a falsifier that induces an almost-surely-correct verifier for regular exponential-family models, i.e. a counterexample-search decision procedure over a parametrized model class.
